deep_dive.title: High-dimensional Embedding Prior for Noisy K-space Domain MRIReconstruction
deep_dive.abstract: Magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) reconstruction under realistic acquisition conditions can be fundamentally viewed as estimating the underlying k-space distribution from incomplete and noise-corrupted measurements. While diffusion models have recently shown strong potential as generative prior for inverse problems,existingapproachesstruggletohandlenoisyreconstruction settings, especially when operating directly in k-space domain. In this work, we propose a unified high-dimensional k-space reconstruction framework tailored for noisy inverse problems, whichenhancesdiffusion-based solversthroughrepresentation lifting.Ratherthanmodifyingthe underlying optimization procedures, the proposed framework augments the data representation space, enabling existing diffusion-based solvers to operate on enriched k-space embeddings with improved expressiveness. Extensive experiments on both in-house and public datasets across varying noise levels and undersampled factors demonstrate that the proposed frame work consistently improves reconstruction quality for multiple diffusion-based inverse solvers. Notably, the largest gains are observed in high-noise regimes, which is consistent with our theoretical analysis of error propagation under high-dimensional representation. These results suggest that high-dimensional representation provides a general and model-agnostic mechanism for improving diffusion-based MRI reconstruction in noisy settings, offering a new perspective on robust k-space generative modeling for practical inverse problems. The code will be available at https://github.com/yqx7150/HEP-MRIRec.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: neuroimaging AI healthcare via MRI reconstruction
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 利用扩散模型进行噪声MRI重建，属于神经影像AI医疗

deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 利用扩散模型进行噪声MRI重建，属于神经影像AI医疗
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a high-dimensional embedding prior framework for noisy k-space MRI reconstruction, enhancing diffusion-based solvers via representation lifting.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有扩散模型在噪声MRI重建中表现不佳。本文提出高维嵌入先验框架，通过表示空间提升增强扩散求解器，统一处理噪声K空间数据。该方法在噪声环境下实现更鲁棒的重建，提升MRI图像质量。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有扩散模型在噪声MRI重建中表现不佳。本文提出高维嵌入先验框架，通过表示空间提升增强扩散求解器，统一处理噪声K空间数据。该方法在噪声环境下实现更鲁棒的重建，提升MRI图像质量。

quick_skim.title: Resolving superposition in AI for interpretability and cross-modal alignment in patient-neuronal images
quick_skim.abstract: Artificial intelligence is transforming our capability to solve biological challenges. In dimensionality bottleneck regimes exacerbated by high-dimensional biological data, neural networks force distinct concepts into the lower dimensions known as superposition. Although this superposition is widely known to hinder interpretability, its impact on corrupting the geometry of latent spaces remains critically overlooked. Here, we utilized sparse autoencoders (SAEs) trained on over 100,000 multiplexed images of patient-derived Parkinson's disease and healthy neurons to resolve superposition. This approach bypasses the mathematical non-uniqueness of feature attribution by shifting to interpretable latent representation analysis. We theoretically and empirically demonstrate that superposition contaminates representational metric spaces, and thereby SAEs successfully recover geometric fidelity. By treating these geometrically purified representations as single-cell state vectors, we adapted single-cell RNA sequencing (scRNA-seq) data analysis methodologies directly to the image domain. Finally, we introduce GW-map, utilizing Gromov-Wasserstein optimal transport to align these image representations with authentic scRNA-seq data de novo. This coupling reconstructs hierarchical neuronal pathology pathways such as Calcium-AIS scaffold, without reference spatial transcriptomics, establishing a scalable foundation for spatial biology. Code is available at https://github.com/jijihihi/Bio\_superposition
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: sparse autoencoders for interpretable latent representations in patient neuronal images
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 稀疏自编码器用于患者神经元图像的可解释潜在表示
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 稀疏自编码器用于患者神经元图像的可解释潜在表示
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 稀疏自编码器用于患者神经元图像的可解释潜在表示
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper uses sparse autoencoders trained on over 100,000 patient-derived neuron images to resolve superposition, enabling interpretable latent representations and cross-modal alignment in Parkinson's disease.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对高维生物数据中神经网络表示叠加损害可解释性的问题，采用稀疏自编码器在超过10万张帕金森病患者神经元图像上学习可解释的潜在表示。该方法绕过了特征归因的非唯一性，提升了潜在空间几何的解释性和跨模态对齐能力。研究为神经影像AI的可解释性提供了实用工具。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文针对高维生物数据中神经网络表示叠加损害可解释性的问题，采用稀疏自编码器在超过10万张帕金森病患者神经元图像上学习可解释的潜在表示。该方法绕过了特征归因的非唯一性，提升了潜在空间几何的解释性和跨模态对齐能力。研究为神经影像AI的可解释性提供了实用工具。
